rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding missing annex blocks or fixing invalid references
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Place implementation-level bindings in the implementation annex when valid
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool for const and eq declarations
Remove guarantees that reference invalid implementation-level paths in type annexes
generic_rules: Add missing annex blocks when validator reports 'missing EOF at annex'
Use only visible identifiers in guarantees and assignments
Place implementation-level bindings in the correct annex scope
Use AGREE scalar types for const and eq declarations
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside annex agree blocks
Use eq for helper equations and guarantee for requirements
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not add guarantees that reference invalid implementation-level paths in type annexes
Do not use unresolved identifiers in guarantees or assignments
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
Do not use invalid AGREE scalar type names in const or eq declarations
